message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: Implementing sort_palette parameter for quantize() method - adds post-quantization palette sorting by brightness/RGB. Need to edit Image.py's quantize method and its tests.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: My feature: add error_threshold parameter to Image.quantize(). Need to edit quantize() method in src/PIL/Image.py and add tests in Tests/test_image_quantize.py. What files does your feature touch? If you also need Image.py, let's coordinate on non-overlapping regions (e.g. different methods) or I can incorporate your quantize-related changes too.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: Our features both touch quantize()'s signature/body, so file-level disjointness isn't possible - let's do sequential turns instead. I'll go first: add a 'sort_palette' kwarg to quantize() (additive, appended near the end of the method after palette generation, plus new test functions appended to the end of test_image_quantize.py). I will NOT touch method= /kmeans/palette params or existing lines. I'll send DONE when finished (should be quick). Then you take the file and add your error_threshold param on top, also additive/appended, avoiding my new lines. Sound good? Reply ACCEPT to confirm order, then wait for my DONE before you start editing.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: Our features (error_threshold vs sort_palette) both touch quantize() in the same files, so a fully disjoint split isn't possible. Proposal: we both edit these files but use Edit (not Write) so we don't clobber, and we sequence our work. I'll go first: add error_threshold param to quantize() signature/docstring/body (small, localized change) and add my tests to test_image_quantize.py, then send DONE. Please wait for my DONE before starting your edits, then add sort_palette as an additional param + your own test additions on top of my changes. That way our diffs compose instead of conflicting. OK?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: Agreed - you go first with error_threshold, send DONE when finished, then I'll add sort_palette on top without touching your new lines.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: Agreed: you go first with sort_palette (additive, appended, not touching method/kmeans/palette params). I will wait for your DONE before starting. I'll then add error_threshold additively on top, also appended, avoiding your new lines. Will wait now.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: There's a mismatch: your first REVISE said you'd go first (sort_palette), my ACCEPT confirmed that, but your latest ACCEPT says I go first (error_threshold). Working tree is still clean (no edits yet from either of us). To resolve: I will go first now since your last message confirms that. I'll add error_threshold additively (new kwarg appended after existing params, new logic appended near end of quantize() body, new test functions appended to end of test_image_quantize.py) and send DONE shortly. Please wait for my DONE before editing.

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: Finished adding error_threshold param to quantize(): new kwarg appended at end of signature, new docstring param, new helper method _quantize_to_error_threshold appended right after quantize(), and 3 new test functions appended to the end of test_image_quantize.py (test_error_threshold_reduces_colors, test_error_threshold_unreachable_uses_max_colors, test_error_threshold_with_palette_raises). I did NOT touch the palette/kmeans/method code paths except adding one guard: 'if palette: if error_threshold is not None: raise ValueError(...)' as the very first line inside the existing 'if palette:' block. All existing tests still pass. The file is yours now to add sort_palette on top - just watch for that one added guard line inside the palette branch.

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image_quantize.py
note: Finished sort_palette feature: added sort_palette param to quantize() (values None/'rgb'/'brightness'), raises ValueError for RGBA palettes or unknown sort_palette values. Implementation is additive - only touched the quantize() signature/docstring and the tail of the method body after 'im = self._new(self.im.quantize(...))' up through the final 'im.palette = ...' line. Also appended 5 new test functions at the end of test_image_quantize.py (after test_transparent_colors_equal). Did not touch method/kmeans/palette-reference code path (the early-return branch for ), so your error_threshold work there should be conflict-free. All 12 quantize tests + broader test_image.py pass. Go ahead and add your changes now.
